bullets.text: Militants and separatists launched coordinated attacks across Mali, with an Al-Qaida-linked group claiming responsibility for strikes on the capital, Bamako, and four other cities.
Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has ordered the army to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah in Lebanon, following strikes that killed six people despite an extended ceasefire.
The US Federal Reserve Chairman Powell stated that a rate cut could be "on the table" as soon as September, indicating that the labor market is not a significant source of inflation pressure.
Two individuals are reported missing after a house fire occurred overnight in New South Wales, north-west of Sydney.
Authorities are investigating a suspected gunman who allegedly targeted Trump administration officials at the White House press dinner.
The Victorian government has pledged $3.5 million to fund security upgrades for nearly 1,700 family violence victim-survivors.

Authorities are investigating the motive of an alleged shooter who targeted US officials at a media event where President Trump was present, marking the third assassination attempt on the president in less than two years.
Following the security breach, President Trump called for legal action against a $400 million White House ballroom project to be dropped, arguing the incident highlights the need for a new ballroom.
Buckingham Palace is reviewing King Charles' security arrangements for his upcoming state visit to the United States in light of the shooting incident at the Washington Hilton.
